mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE is below its 10-year moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and investor confidence, which drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances where RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and unemployment is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment combined with a strong economy, highly supportive of equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are flat or negative (average return < 0.01%) in periods where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE meet the low thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a sharp decline in industrial production signals worsening economic conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances during identified economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate robust construction activity and consumer confidence in the economy's future, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as investment flows into related sectors.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when HOUST is consistently above 1000 units.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive or flat (average return >= 0%) in periods of high CPI growth exceeding 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, indicated by strong non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and consumer base, which underpins economic growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 30% of periods with strong PAYEMS growth exceeding 150,000 average monthly change.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth signifies a strong overall economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when GDPC1 year-over-year growth consistently exceeds 2.5%.
